[title] JUNO

[author_names] By Diablo Cody

[notes] REVISED PINK
[draft_date] -- FEBRUARY 06, 2007
[notes] FULL BLUE
[draft_date] --  JANUARY 22, 2007
[notes] PRODUCTION WHITE
[draft_date] --  JANUARY 12, 2007

[notes] Production Office:
[notes] Dancing Elk Pictures Ltd.
[contact] 214-2400 Boundary Road
[notes] Burnaby, BC  V5M 3Z3

[scene_heading] EXT. CENTENNIAL LANE - DUSK
[page_number] 1
[page_number] 1

[action] JUNO MacGUFF stands on a placid street in a nondescript 
[action] subdivision, facing the curb. It’s FALL. Juno is sixteen years 
[action] old, an artfully bedraggled burnout kid. She winces and shields 
[action] her eyes from the glare of the sun. The object of her rapt 
[action] attention is a battered living room set, abandoned curbside by 
[action] its former owners. There is a fetid-looking leather recliner, a 
[action] chrome-edged coffee table, and a tasteless latchhooked rug 
[action] featuring a roaring tiger. 

[character] JUNO V.O.
[dialogue] It started with a chair.

[scene_heading] INT. BLEEKER HOUSE - MOLD-O’-RIFFIC BASEMENT- NIGHT 
[page_number] 2
[page_number] 2

[action] FLASHBACK - Juno approaches a boy hidden by shadow. He’s 
[action] sitting in an overstuffed chair. She slowly, clumsily lowers 
[action] herself onto his lap. 

[action] A  60’s Brazilian track plays from a vintage record player. 

[character] WHISPERED VOICE
[dialogue] Do you know how long I’ve wanted 
[dialogue] this?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Yeah.

[character] WHISPERED VOICE
[dialogue] Wizard.

[scene_heading] EXT. CENTENNIAL LANE - CONTINUED
[page_number] 3
[page_number] 3

[action] A DOG barks, jarring Juno back to reality.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Quiet, Banana. Hey, shut your gob 
[dialogue] for a second, okay?

[action] We see a teacup poodle tethered in the yard a few feet away 
[action] from the abandoned living room set. The dog yaps again.

[character] JUNO (V.O.)
[dialogue] This is the most magnificent discarded 
[dialogue] living room set I’ve ever seen. 

[action] She swigs from an absurdly oversized carton of juice and 
[action] wipes her mouth with the back of her hand.

[action] BEGIN ANIMATED TITLE SEQUENCE:

[] (CONTINUED)

[action] Juno marching down various street, pumping her arms like a 
[action] jogger and chugging intermittently from the huge carton of 
[action] juice. We watch her breathlessly navigate suburbia, clearly 
[action] on a mission.

[subheading] OMITTED
[page_number] 4
[page_number] 4

[subheading] OMITTED
[page_number] 5
[page_number] 5

[scene_heading] EXT. DRUGSTORE - DAY
[page_number] 6
[page_number] 6

[action] Finally, a panting Juno arrives at DANCING ELK DRUG on the 
[action] main drag of her small Minnesota suburb, Dancing Elk. 

[action] The automatic doors of the store part to reveal Juno’s 
[action] flushed serious face. She carelessly flings the empty juice 
[action] container over her shoulder and onto the curb. A group of 
[action] DROPOUTS with skateboards near the entrance glare at her.

[action] She enters the DRUGSTORE.

[scene_heading] INT. DRUGSTORE - CONTINUOUS
[page_number] 7
[page_number] 7

[action] ROLLO, the eccentric drugstore clerk, sneers at Juno from 
[action] behind the counter. He wears a polyester uniform vest.

[character] ROLLO
[dialogue] Well, well. If it isn’t MacGuff the 
[dialogue] Crime Dog! Back for another test?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I think the last one was defective. 
[dialogue] The plus sign looked more like a 
[dialogue] division sign.

[action] Rollo regards her with intense skepticism.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I remain unconvinced. 

[action] Rollo pulls the bathroom key out of reach.

[character] ROLLO
[dialogue] This is your third test today, Mama 
[dialogue] Bear. Your eggo is preggo, no doubt 
[dialogue] about it! 

[action] An eavesdropping TOUGH GIRL wearing an oversized jacket and 
[action] lots of makeup gapes at Juno from the beauty aisle.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            2.
[] CONTINUED:
[page_number] 3
[page_number] 3

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] TOUGH GIRL
[dialogue] Three times? Oh girl, you are  way  
[dialogue] pregnant. It’s easy to tell. Is 
[dialogue] your nipples real brown?

[action] A pile of stolen COSMETICS falls out of the girl’s jacket and 
[action] clatters to the floor.

[character] TOUGH GIRL
[dialogue] Balls!

[action] Juno crosses and crosses her legs awkwardly, hopping. It’s 
[action] obvious she has to use the bathroom urgently.

[character] ROLLO 
[dialogue] Maybe you’re having twins. Maybe 
[dialogue] your little boyfriend’s got mutant 
[dialogue] sperms and he knocked you up twice!

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Silencio! I just drank my weight in 
[dialogue] Sunny D. and I have to go, pronto. 

[action] Rollo sighs and slips her the bathroom key. Juno races down 
[action] one of the aisles.

[character] ROLLO
[dialogue] Well, you know where the lavatory is.
[parenthetical] (Calling after her) )
[dialogue] You pay for that pee stick when 
[dialogue] you’re done! Don’t think it’s yours 
[dialogue] just because you’ve marked it with 
[dialogue] your urine!

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Jesus, I didn’t say it was.

[character] ROLLO
[dialogue] Well, it’s not. You’re not a lion 
[dialogue] in a pride!
[parenthetical] (to himself)
[dialogue] These kids, acting like lions with 
[dialogue] their unplanned pregnancies and 
[dialogue] their Sunny Delights.

[scene_heading] INT.  DRUGSTORE - BATHROOM - DAY
[page_number] 8
[page_number] 8

[action] In the dim, reeking public bathroom, Juno hovers over the 
[action] commode with her boxer shorts around her ankles. She clumsily 
[action] tries to use the pregnancy test.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            3.
[] CONTINUED:
[page_number] 7
[page_number] 7

[] (CONTINUED)

[action] We see the test box sitting on the sink. It’s a TeenWave 
[action] Discount Pregnancy Test. The accompanying outdated package 
[action] photo is of a shrugging 80s teen with a resigned expression. 
[action] The fine print on the box reads “From the makers of Sun-Glitz 
[action] Lightening Hair Spritz!”

[scene_heading] INT. DRUGSTORE - FRONT COUNTER - DAY
[page_number] 9
[page_number] 9

[action] Juno holds the developing test in her hand and slaps the open 
[action] test box on the front counter. Rollo scans it and bags it 
[action] indifferently.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Oh, and this too.

[action] She places a giant licorice Super Rope on the counter.

[character] ROLLO
[dialogue] So what’s the prognosis, Fertile 
[dialogue] Myrtle? Minus or plus? 

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] (examining stick) I don’t know. 
[dialogue] It’s not...seasoned yet. Wait. Huh. 
[dialogue] Yeah, there’s that pink plus sign 
[dialogue] again. God, it’s unholy.

[action] She shakes the stick desperately in an attempt to skew the 
[action] results. Shake. Shake. Nothing.

[character] ROLLO
[dialogue] That ain’t no Etch-a-Sketch. This 
[dialogue] is one doodle that can’t be undid, 
[dialogue] homeskillet.

[scene_heading] EXT. MACGUFF HOUSE - EVENING
[page_number] 10
[page_number] 10

[action] Juno walks slowly and dejectedly up the street to her house, 
[action] gnawing on the Super Rope. She stops and loops the Super Rope 
[action] over a low-hanging tree branch, contemplating how to fashion 
[action] a noose.

[action] Juno trudges toward her HOUSE. The yard is a wild tangle of 
[action] prairie grass and wild flowers.

[page_number] 11

[page_number] 12

[page_number] 8

[scene_heading] INT.  MACGUFF HOUSE - JUNO’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
[page_number] 14
[page_number] 14

[action] Juno’s BEDROOM is decorated with punk posters: The Damned, 
[action] The Germs, the Stooges, Television, Richard Hell, etc.

[action] She picks up a hamburger-shaped phone to call her best 
[action] friend, LEAH.

[subheading] OMITTED
[page_number] 15
[page_number] 15

[subheading] OMITTED
[page_number] 16
[page_number] 16

[scene_heading] INT. LEAH’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
[page_number] 17
[page_number] 17

[action] LEAH’s room is cluttered with the sentimental junk that 
[action] certain girls love to hoard. The PHONE rings.

[character] LEAH
[parenthetical] (answering phone)
[dialogue] Yo-yo-yiggity-yo.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I am a suicide risk.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Is this Juno?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] No it’s Morgan Freeman. Got any 
[dialogue] bones that need collecting?

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Only the one in my pants.

[character] JUNO
[parenthetical] (in low tones)
[dialogue] Dude, I’m pregnant.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Maybe it’s just a food baby. Did 
[dialogue] you have a big lunch?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] It’s not a food baby. I took three 
[dialogue] pregnancy tests today. I am 
[dialogue] definitely up the spout.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            5.

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] How did you even generate enough 
[dialogue] pee for three pregnancy tests?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I drank like ten tons of Sunny 
[dialogue] Delight. Anyway, yeah. I’m 
[dialogue] pregnant. And you’re shockingly 
[dialogue] cavalier. 

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Is this for real? Like  for real, 
[dialogue] for real?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Unfortunately, yes.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Oh my God! Oh shit! Phuket 
[dialogue] Thailand!

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] That’s the kind of emotion I was 
[dialogue] looking for in the first take.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Well, are you going to go to 
[dialogue] Havenbrooke or Women Now for the 
[dialogue] abortion? You need a note from your 
[dialogue] parents for Havenbrooke.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I know. Women Now, I guess. The 
[dialogue] commercial says they help women now.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Want me to call for you? I called 
[dialogue] for Becky last year.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Eh, I’ll call them myself. But I do 
[dialogue] need your help with something very 
[dialogue] urgent. 

[scene_heading] EXT. CENTENNIAL LANE - NIGHT
[page_number] 18
[page_number] 18

[action] Leah and Juno struggle to drag a recliner across a well-
[action] manicured suburban lawn. They make a formidable team.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Heavy lifting can only help you at 
[dialogue] this point.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            6.
[] CONTINUED:
[page_number] 17
[page_number] 17

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] That is sick, man.

[action] Leah busts a gut laughing. It’s a stunningly accurate 
[action] portrayal of Bleeker’s parents.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] So, you were bored? Is that how 
[dialogue] this blessed miracle came to be?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Nah, it was a premeditated act. The 
[dialogue] sex, I mean, not getting pregnant.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] When did you decide you were going 
[dialogue] to do Bleeker?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Like, a year ago, in Spanish class.

[scene_heading] INT. DANCING ELK SCHOOL - SPANISH CLASS - DAY - (FLASHBACK)
[page_number] 19
[page_number] 19

[action] Bleeker and Juno are sitting at their desks, listening to a 
[action] teacher lecturing about spanish. Bleeker discreetly pushes a 
[action] POSTCARD to Juno with his foot. She picks it up off the 
[action] floor, reads it, then looks at Bleeker, who is watching the 
[action] teacher obediently.

[scene_heading] EXT. CENTENNIAL LANE - NIGHT
[page_number] 20
[page_number] 20

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] A ha ! You love him.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] It’s extremely complicated, and I’d 
[dialogue] rather not talk about it in my 
[dialogue] fragile state.

[action] She hefts a coffee table with her bare hands. She’s wearing 
[action] her father’s LIFTING BELT.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] So, what was it like humping 
[dialogue] Bleeker’s bony bod?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] It was magnificent, man!

[scene_heading] INT. BLEEKER’S BEDROOM - MORNING
[page_number] 24

[action] CU of Bleeker putting on double socks in his Car-Bed.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN - BLEEKER’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING
[page_number] 25
[page_number] 25

[action] CU of a CROISSANT POCKET warming in the microwave.

[scene_heading] EXT. BLEEKER HOUSE - MORNING
[page_number] 26
[page_number] 26

[action] PAUL BLEEKER steps onto the front porch of his house for 
[action] early morning track practice. He wears a cross country 
[action] uniform that reads “DANCING ELK CONDORS.” He is eating some 
[action] kind of microwaved snack gimmick.

[action] Bleeker is startled to discover that Juno is outside waiting 
[action] for him. She has somehow arranged the living room set on the 
[action] front lawn, and is seated in the armchair, chewing a pipe 
[action] officiously.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Hey Bleek.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] Hey, cool tiger. Looks proud.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Yeah, I swiped it from Ms. Rancick.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] Cool.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Your shorts are looking especially 
[dialogue] gold today.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] My mom uses color-safe bleach.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            8.

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Go Carole.
[parenthetical] (a beat)
[dialogue] So, guess what?

[character] BLEEKER
[parenthetical] (shrugs)
[dialogue] I don’t know...

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I’m pregnant.

[action] Stunned silence. Juno pops up the footrest of the recliner 
[action] and leans back comfortably.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] I guess so. 
[parenthetical] (fidgeting)
[dialogue] What are you going to do?

[action] The Dancing Elk Prep cross country team runs past Bleeker’s 
[action] house in a thundering herd, wearing a motley assortment of 
[action] warm-ups. Their momentum stirs the crackling fall leaves. 
[action] They wave and holler at Bleeker and Juno.

[character] JUNO V.O.
[dialogue] When I see them all running like 
[dialogue] that, with their  things  bouncing 
[dialogue] around in their shorts, I always 
[dialogue] picture them naked, even if I don’t 
[dialogue] want to. I have intrusive thoughts 
[dialogue] all the time. 

[subheading] OMITTED
[page_number] 27
[page_number] 27

[scene_heading] EXT. BLEEKER HOUSE - CONTINUED
[page_number] 28
[page_number] 28

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] I’m supposed to be running.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I know.

[action] There’s an awkward silence.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] So, what do you think we should  
[dialogue] do? 
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            9.
[] CONTINUED:
[page_number] 26
[page_number] 26

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I thought I might, you know, nip it 
[dialogue] in the bud before it gets worse. 
[dialogue] Because I heard in health class that 
[dialogue] pregnancy often results in an infant. 

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] Yeah, typically. That’s what 
[dialogue] happens when our moms and teachers 
[dialogue] get pregnant.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] So that’s cool with you, then?

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] Yeah, wizard, I guess. I mean do 
[dialogue] what you think is right.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I’m real sorry I had sex with you. 
[dialogue] I know it wasn’t your idea.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] Whose idea was it? 

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I’ll see you at school, O.K.?

[action] She mounts her bicycle and waves before riding off.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] (to nobody in particular) 
[dialogue] Whose idea was it?

[scene_heading] EXT. DANCING ELK SCHOOL - DAY
[page_number] 29
[page_number] 29

[action] Juno pushes her crappy bike into the bike rack and winds a 
[action] lock around it. In the background, a group of 3 NERDS play a 
[action] live-action RPG.

[character] NERD    
[dialogue] You did not! You don’t have the 
[dialogue] armor. That Orc Armor you bought 
[dialogue] from the wizard doesn’t have the 
[dialogue] power level to parry my hit!

[scene_heading] INT. DANCING ELK SCHOOL - CORRIDOR - DAY
[page_number] 29A
[page_number] 29A

[action] Juno tries to push through the masses, but the throng of 
[action] students is thick and unwielding.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            10.

[scene_heading] INT. DANCING ELK SCHOOL - HALLWAY - DAY
[page_number] 30
[page_number] 30

[action] Juno rummages through her locker, which is plastered with 
[action] photos of Leah and Bleeker, plus a giant poster of Iggy Pop 
[action] in his heyday.

[action] She grabs a dilapidated physics textbook. A few pages slip 
[action] out. STEVE RENDAZO (the same asshole who harassed her as she 
[action] walked to the drugstore) passes by in the hallway.

[character] STEVE RENDAZO
[dialogue] Hey, your book fell apart!

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Yeah.

[character] STEVE RENDAZO
[dialogue] It must have looked at your face. 
[dialogue] PWAH!

[action] He high-fives his klatch of buddies and moves along.

[character] JUNO V.O.
[dialogue] The funny thing is that Steve Rendazo 
[dialogue] secretly wants me. Jocks like him 
[dialogue] always want freaky girls. Girls with 
[dialogue] horn-rimmed glasses and vegan 
[dialogue] footwear and Goth makeup. Girls who 
[dialogue] play the cello and wear Converse All-
[dialogue] Stars and want to be children’s 
[dialogue] librarians when they grow up. Oh 
[dialogue] yeah, jocks eat that shit up. 

[action] We see Steve looking back at Juno for a brief second with 
[action] mixed feelings.

[character] JUNO V.O.
[dialogue] They just won’t admit it, because 
[dialogue] they’re supposed to be into perfect 
[dialogue] cheerleaders like Leah. Who, 
[dialogue] incidentally, is into teachers.

[action] We see Leah at the far end of the hallway, talking animatedly 
[action] with a paunchy middle-aged teacher, KEITH.

[character] LEAH
[parenthetical] (from a distance)
[dialogue] Me too! I  love  Woody Allen!
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            11.

[scene_heading] INT.  DANCING ELK SCHOOL - SCIENCE LAB - DAY
[page_number] 31
[page_number] 31

[action] STUDENTS bustle in, as the teacher, MR. TINKER tries to 
[action] maintain order. Juno heads toward her desk and sets down her 
[action] bag.

[character] MR. TINKER
[dialogue] People! We’re doing our 
[dialogue] photomagnetism lab today, so find 
[dialogue] your partner and break out into 
[dialogue] fours. 

[action] Juno looks up and meets eyes with her longtime lab partner: 
[action] Bleeker. Sound the gong of awkwardness!

[action] Juno and Bleeker head separately over to an available lab 
[action] station and unpack their bags in silence.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Well! Nothing like experimenting.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] I did the prep questions for this 
[dialogue] lab last night. You can copy my 
[dialogue] answers if you need to. 

[action] He slides a piece of graph paper in front of Juno without 
[action] looking at her.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Oh, I couldn’t copy your work.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] But you copy my work every week.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Oh yeah. I’m kind of a deadbeat lab 
[dialogue] partner, huh?

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] I don’t mind. You definitely bring 
[dialogue] something to the table. 

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Charisma?

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] Or something.

[action] The other two LAB PARTNERS, a humorless couple, join them at 
[action] the station.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            12.

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] So, who’s ready for some 
[dialogue] photomagnificence?

[character] GIRL LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] I have a menstrual migraine, and I 
[dialogue] can’t look at bright lights today.

[character] GUY LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] Amanda, I told you to go to the 
[dialogue] infirmary and lie down. You never 
[dialogue] listen.

[character] GIRL LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] No Josh, I don’t take orders. Not 
[dialogue] from you and not from any man. 

[character] GUY LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] You know, you’ve been acting like 
[dialogue] this ever since I went up to see my 
[dialogue] brother at Mankato. I told you, 
[dialogue] nothing happened!

[character] GIRL LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] Something happened. Because your 
[dialogue] eyes? Are very cold? They’re very 
[dialogue] cold, Josh. They’re cold, lying eyes. 

[character] GUY LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] What? My eyes are not lying!

[character] GIRL LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] Yes they are, Josh. Since Mankato, 
[dialogue] they have been lying eyes.

[action] Juno and Bleeker observe the argument like tennis spectators, 
[action] fascinated by the dynamics of a real couple.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] Okay...I’m going to set up the 
[dialogue] apparatus. Juno, want to get a C-
[dialogue] clamp out of that drawer?

[character] GIRL LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] I’m going to the infirmary.

[character] GUY LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] Good. Call me when you’re OFF the 
[dialogue] rag.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            13.
[] CONTINUED:
[page_number] 31
[page_number] 31

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] GIRL LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] Fine. Call me when you learn how to 
[dialogue] love just one person and not cheat 
[dialogue] at your brother’s college just 
[dialogue] because you had four Smirnoff Ices 
[dialogue] and a bottle of Snow Peak Peach 
[dialogue] flavored Boone’s! 

[character] GUY LAB PARTNER
[dialogue] Good, I’ll be sure to do that, 
[dialogue] Amanda. I’ll make a note of it.

[action] He furiously scrawls a fake memo in his notebook.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Snow Peak Peach  is  the best flavor 
[dialogue] of Boone’s. Right, Bleek?

[action] Bleeker reddens and continues constructing the apparatus.

[action] GIRL LAB PARTNER stalks off dramatically.

[action] Bleeker shakes his head and rifles through his textbook.

[scene_heading] INT.  MACGUFF HOUSE - JUNO’S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
[page_number] 32
[page_number] 32

[action] Juno examines a large ad in the newspaper that depicts a 
[action] distraught TEEN GIRL clutching her head in a moment of staged 
[action] conflict. The ad reads: “Pregnant? Find the clinic that gives 
[action] women choice. Women’s Choice Health Center.”

[action] Juno picks up her hamburger phone and dials. For a moment, 
[action] she attempts to copy the melodramatic pose from the ad, 
[action] checking herself out in the mirror.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] (talking along with voice prompt) 
[dialogue] “Para instruciones en Espanol, 
[dialogue] oprima numero dos.”

[action] She presses a few buttons in succession.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Yes, hello, I need to procure a hasty 
[dialogue] abortion?...What was that? I’m sorry, 
[dialogue] I’m on my hamburger phone and it’s

[action] thumbnail and spits it onto the floor.

[action] woman, who absently scratches her arm with long fake nails.

[action] clicks her nails on the front desk. Another woman blows on 
[action] her fresh manicure. Everyone seems to be fidgeting with their 
[action] fingers somehow. Juno suddenly looks terror-stricken...

[action] drift shut. Juno’s figure recedes into the distance as she 
[action] tears off down the street, running as fast as she can.

[action] McMansion. The entire yard is unlandscaped soil. Mac presses 
[action] the doorbell while Juno chews her nails uncomfortably. Both 
[action] look mortified as they wait for someone to greet them.

[action] her early thirties. Very Banana Republic.

[action] Juno and Mac exit. Mark shuts the door. All is silent in the 
[action] foyer. Mark, Vanessa and Gerta stand motionless. Gerta pumps her 
[action] fist triumphantly, trying to create a mood of celebration.

[character] GERTA
[parenthetical] (overly aggressive)
[dialogue] All RIGHT!

[action] Vanessa buries her head in her hands and weeps hoarsely.

[scene_heading] EXT. SUBURBAN STREETS - MORNING
[page_number] 61
[page_number] 61

[dialogue] I just know he doesn’t like Katrina 
[dialogue] and I don’t think he should toy 
[dialogue] with her emotions like that. She 
[dialogue] seems so nice and all.

[character] LEAH
[dialogue] Okay Juno, I’m really convinced.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Prom is for wenises, anyway. Once 
[dialogue] you’re old enough to go, it’s not 
[dialogue] cool anymore.

[action] Dr. Pepper Lip Smacker and backs out of the driveway.

[page_number] 90
[page_number] 90

[action] website. He has the blank expression of a bored obsessive. 
[action] The doorbell rings.

[page_number] 91
[page_number] 91

[action] knocked-up. She holds a stack of CDs. Mark breaks into a 
[action] grin.

[dialogue] Wow. That shirt is working hard.

[character] JUNO
[parenthetical] (furtive)

[dialogue] Nope. We’re safe.

[dialogue] Cool.

[character] MARK
[dialogue] Come on, I have something for you.

[scene_heading] INT. LORING HOUSE - BASEMENT - AFTERNOON
[page_number] 92
[page_number] 92

[action] unattractive, much like Paulie Bleeker’s. Mark pulls a chain 
[action] to illuminate a bare bulb.

[scene_heading] INT. LORING HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY  
[page_number] 93

[action] Juno reaches the top of the stairs and scrambles toward the 
[action] front door, only to be intercepted by Vanessa, who’s 
[action] returning home from work, carrying her briefcase and a 
[action] freshly purchased NURSING PILLOW. They nearly collide.

[character] VANESSA
[dialogue] Juno? What’s going on? 

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Nothing.

[action] It’s obvious from Juno’s tears and flushed face that she’s 
[action] lying. Vanessa instantly goes pale with fear, but she tries 
[action] her hardest to seem serene and “together” in front of Juno.

[character] VANESSA
[dialogue] (pretending to be calm)
[dialogue] Mark? Why is Juno crying?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I’m not crying. I’m allergic to 
[dialogue] fine home furnishings. See you 
[dialogue] later.

[dialogue] just part of the whole process.

[action] looks at Juno’s expression and knows Mark is lying.

[dialogue] ever and then - boom - Two weeks 
[dialogue] later, she’s in our living room.

[scene_heading] EXT. LORING HOUSE - AFTERNOON
[page_number] 94
[page_number] 94

[action] Juno runs up to her car sobbing. She struggles with the keys, 
[action] but finally makes it into the Previa and drives off.

[scene_heading] EXT. HIGHWAY - AFTERNOON

[action] The Previa slides off the road and comes to a stop on the 
[action] shoulder.

[scene_heading] INT. PREVIA - AFTERNOON

[action] Juno buckles over the steering wheel, crying, unwinding for 
[action] the first time since she became pregnant. 

[action] After a beat, she begins to gather herself.

[scene_heading] INT. BLEEKER’S BEDROOM - NIGHT

[action] Bleeker is sitting next to his bed, noodling on the guitar. 
[action] He’s playing a theme that we will soon recognise.

[scene_heading] EXT. CORNER STORE - NIGHT

[action] Juno lays on the hood of her Previa, contemplating her 
[action] future. We push in close... when she gets an idea.

[action] She hops off the hood and scurries into the Previa where she 
[action] finds a crumpled up Jiffy Lube receipt. She unfolds it and 
[action] pulls out a pen, ready to write something... a note?

[scene_heading] EXT. LORING HOUSE - NIGHT

[action] It’s quiet after the storm. Inside, we see Vanessa sitting 
[action] alone at her perfect dining room table, drinking a glass of 
[action] wine.

[scene_heading] INT.  LORING HOUSE - DINING ROOM - NIGHT
[page_number] 103
[page_number] 103

[action] Vanessa takes a sip and continues to let the days events sink 
[action] in. After a beat, Mark comes down the stairs to join her.

[character] MARK
[dialogue] I called Gerta Rauss. She says she 
[dialogue] can represent both of us. They call 
[dialogue] it “collaborative divorce.” It’s 
[dialogue] apparently all the rage right now. 
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            86.

[] (MORE)

[] (CONTINUED)

[] And it’s easy because we don’t have 
[] children.

[character] VANESSA
[dialogue] No, it’s fine. Thanks for making 
[dialogue] the call, I guess.

[action] Mark nods and sits down.

[character] VANESSA
[dialogue] We’re actually, finally doing this?

[character] MARK
[dialogue] Looks like it, yeah.

[character] VANESSA
[dialogue] Have you found a place to stay?

[character] MARK
[dialogue] Yeah, downtown.

[character] VANESSA
[dialogue] A hotel?

[character] MARK
[dialogue] It’s a loft.

[character] VANESSA
[parenthetical] (lightly teasing)
[dialogue] Aren’t you the cool guy?

[action] They STARE at the wall, speechless and defeated.

[character] VANESSA
[dialogue] I wanted a baby so bad. So bad.

[action] She buries her head in her hands.

[character] MARK
[dialogue] I know you did.

[action] There’s a LOUD KNOCK on the front door.

[scene_heading] EXT. LORING HOUSE - ENTRY - NIGHT  

[action] Mark opens the door. There’s a folded piece of paper sitting 
[action] on the doormat. He squints and sees Juno pulling away in the 
[action] van.

[action] Mark carefully unfolds the piece of paper--it takes a minute 
[action] because of Juno’s proficiency in “teen girl origami.” He 
[action] holds it up. We can see there’s WRITING on the back.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            87.
[] CONTINUED:
[page_number] 103
[page_number] 103
[] MARK (cont'd)

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] MARK
[dialogue] It looks like a bill from Jiffy-
[dialogue] Lube.

[action] Vanessa takes the note from his hand and turns it over, 
[action] examining it.

[character] VANESSA
[dialogue] It’s for me.

[scene_heading] EXT. MACGUFF HOUSE - NIGHT
[page_number] 95
[page_number] 95

[action] Juno parks her car and walks up to her house. A porch light’s 
[action] been left on for her, and the place looks cozy and inviting.

[character] JUNO V.O.
[dialogue] I never realize how much I like being 
[dialogue] home unless I’ve been somewhere 
[dialogue] really different for a while.

[action] She picks a CROCUS from the unkempt garden near the porch and 
[action] sniffs it. She lifts her shirt and tickles her belly with it. 
[action] Then she tucks the flower into her unkempt hair.

[scene_heading] INT.  MACGUFF HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT
[page_number] 96
[page_number] 96

[action] Mac is alone at the kitchen table going over the family 
[action] finances while drinking one of Bren’s weight loss shakes.

[action] Juno enters.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Hi Dad.

[character] MAC
[dialogue] Hey, big puffy version of Junebug. 
[dialogue] Where have you been?

[character] JUNO

[dialogue] maturity level. Where is everyone?

[character] MAC
[dialogue] Bren took Liberty Bell to her tot 
[dialogue] ice skating class.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Tot ice skating? Tots can’t ice 
[dialogue] skate. Liberty Bell’s still getting 
[dialogue] the hang of stairs.
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            88.
[] CONTINUED:

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] MAC
[dialogue] No, but you know Bren. She dreams big.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Yeah, she does. 

[character] MAC
[dialogue] You look a little morose, honey. 
[dialogue] What’s eating you?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] I’m losing my faith in humanity.

[character] MAC
[dialogue] Think you can narrow it down for me.

[character] JUNO

[dialogue] people ever stay together for good.

[character] MAC
[dialogue] You mean like couples?

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Yeah, like people in love.

[character] MAC
[dialogue] Are you having boy trouble? I gotta be 
[dialogue] honest; I don’t much approve of you 
[dialogue] dating in your condition, ‘cause... well, 
[dialogue] that’s kind of messed up.

[character] JUNO

[character] MAC

[dialogue] that what you girls call it? 
[dialogue] Skanky? Skeevy?

[dialogue] Tore up from the floor up?

[dialogue] to know that it’s possible for two 
[dialogue] people to stay happy together 
[dialogue] forever. Or at least for a few years.

[page_number] 96
[page_number] 96

[] (CONTINUED)

[character] MAC

[dialogue] record in the world, but I have 
[dialogue] been with your stepmother for ten 
[dialogue] years now, and I’m proud to say 
[dialogue] that we’re very happy.

[character] MAC (CONT’D)

[dialogue] can do is to find a person who 
[dialogue] loves you for exactly what you are.

[dialogue] handsome, what have you, the right

[dialogue] sun shines out your ass. That’s the

[scene_heading] EXT. BLEEKER HOUSE - MORNING 
[page_number] 101

[action] He looks down to see a message scrawled in chalk on the 
[action] stoop: “BLEEKER- CHECK THE MAIL.”

[action] He walks down to the end of the driveway and opens the latch 
[action] on the mailbox.

[action] At  least  one hundred containers of ORANGE TIC TACS come pouring 
[action] out in an colorful deluge. They spill out onto the driveway. 

[action] Bleeker smiles.

[scene_heading] EXT. DANCING ELK SCHOOL - TRACK - MORNING
[page_number] 102

[action] and jogs up to the chainlink fence.

[action] a wall. Juno feigns whiplash. 

[action] Bren appears behind them and orders Juno out of the 
[action] wheelchair, exasperated. She pulls off Juno’s surgical cap. 
[action] Leah and Juno snicker.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL - BIRTHING SUITE - DAY
[page_number] 112
[page_number] 112

[action] Juno is curled up on her bed in the birthing room. The birth 
[action] is obviously over; there’s that air of stillness and 
[action] accomplishment, a task completed. The doctors have cleared 
[action] out. Mac sits in a chair next to the bed, looking like he’s 
[action] not sure what to say.

[action] He holds up a deck of Playing cards as if to initiate a game, 
[action] but Juno gently pushes his hand away. Their eyes meet. His 
[action] expression is helpless, hers is utterly drained.

[character] MAC
[dialogue] Someday, you’ll be back here, 
[dialogue] honey. On your terms.

[action] Juno nods and swallows.

[action] Over Mac’s shoulder, a silhouette appears in the doorway. 
[action] It’s Bleeker in his track uniform. 

[action] Juno sees him and bursts into sudden, ragged sobs. Mac 
[action] glances at her, alarmed. Paulie moves toward the bed. Mac 
[action] clears his throat, gets up and leaves the room.

[action] Paulie climbs onto the hospital bed and carefully puts his 
[action] arm around Juno. She leans into him, letting herself go for 
[action] the first time in months.

[scene_heading] INT. LORING HOUSE - NURSERY - DAY
[page_number] 116

[action] The nursery is perfectly decorated in Vanessa’s immaculate 
[action] taste. Nothing looks as though it’s been touched. We see the 
[action] antique ROCKING CHAIR sitting empty.

[character] JUNO V.O.
[dialogue] It ended with a chair.

[action] We pan past the wall Vanessa had said “needed something.” 
[action] There’s a framed note on the wall. It looks like it was 
[action] handwritten on the back of a Jiffy Lube bill. We see that it 
[action] says: “Vanessa - If you’re still in, I’m still in. Juno.”

[action] We move to the door and get a view of Vanessa’s bedroom. We 
[action] see her lying in bed with a burp cloth draped over her 
[action] shoulder, feeding the baby. For the first time ever, Vanessa 
[action] looks disorganized, unshowered--and incredibly happy. There 
[action] are formula bottles on the bedside table and a bassinet 
[action] pushed close to the bed. Obviously, she hasn’t been away from 
[action] the baby for a single moment. 

[action] The baby reaches up and grabs Vanessa’s thumb. Vanessa glows 
[action] with an expression of pure bliss.

[scene_heading] EXT. MACGUFF HOUSE - DAY
[page_number] 119
[page_number] 119

[action] It’s a sparkling summer afternoon. Juno wheels her bicycle 
[action] out of the detached garage. She waves goodbye to Bren, who is 
[action] playing in the yard with two WEIMARANERS. She looks almost as 
[action] happy with her dogs as VANESSA looked with her baby.

[scene_heading] EXT. SUBURBAN STREETS - DAY
[page_number] 120
[page_number] 120

[action] Juno rides her bike aggressively down the street. Her guitar 
[action] is slung over her shoulder in a gig bag. She’s obviously not 
[action] pregnant anymore. She looks happy, but older.

[character] JUNO V.O. 
[dialogue] As boyfriends go, Paulie Bleeker is 
[dialogue] totally boss. He is the cheese to 
[dialogue] my macaroni. I know people are 
[dialogue] supposed to fall in love  before 
[dialogue] they reproduce, but normalcy’s not 
[dialogue] really our style. 

[action] As she tears recklessly down the street on her bikes, she 
[action] passes the Dancing Elk TRACK TEAM, still running in 
[action] outrageously skimpy shorts and bandanas.

[action] Juno RIDES up to a garage where Bleeker is sitting, tuning 
[action] his guitar. 

[scene_heading] EXT. BLEEKER HOUSE - DAY
[page_number] 121
[page_number] 121

[action] Juno bikes up to find Bleeker sitting on the stoop. She 
[action] smiles and takes out her guitar. She sits across from Bleeker 
[action] and pulls the pick out of the strings.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Ready?

[action] Bleeker nods.

[action] Juno begins strumming her guitar and playing “Anyone Else But 
[action] You,” by the Moldy Peaches. Bleeker joins in. At first their 
[action] playing is discordant, but suddenly it works.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] You’re a part time lover and a full-
[dialogue] time friend. The monkey on your 
[dialogue] back is the latest trend. 
[action] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            100.

[] (MORE)

[] (CONTINUED)

[] I don’t see what anyone can see, in 
[] anyone else but you.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] Here is the church and here is the 
[dialogue] steeple. We sure are cute for two 
[dialogue] ugly people. I don’t see what anyone 
[dialogue] can see, in anyone else but you.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] We both have shiny happy fits of 
[dialogue] rage. You want more fans, I want 
[dialogue] more stage. I don’t see what anyone 
[dialogue] can see, in anyone else but you.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] You are always trying to keep it 
[dialogue] real. I’m in love with how you 
[dialogue] feel. I don’t see what anyone can 
[dialogue] see, in anyone else but you.

[character] BLEEKER
[dialogue] I kiss you on the brain in the shadow 
[dialogue] of a train. I kiss you all starry-
[dialogue] eyed, my body’s swinging from side to 
[dialogue] side. I don’t see what anyone can 
[dialogue] see, in anyone else but you.

[character] JUNO
[dialogue] The pebbles forgive me, the trees 
[dialogue] forgive me. So why can’t you 
[dialogue] forgive me? I don’t see what anyone 
[dialogue] can see, in anyone else but you.

[character] JUNO AND BLEEKER TOGETHER
[dialogue] Du du du du du du dudu. Du du du du 
[dialogue] du du dudu. I don’t see what anyone 
[dialogue] can see, in anyone else but you.

[action] She and Bleeker exchange glances as they play. They smile 
[action] ambiguously. Juno leans over and kisses Bleeker on the cheek.

[action] Pull out to reveal the surrounding green suburb buzzing with 
[action] life and summer activity.

[character (DUAL)]  FADE TO BLACK
[dialogue (DUAL)] Juno - Production Revised Yellow - 3/05/07            101.
[] CONTINUED:
[page_number (DUAL)] 121
[page_number (DUAL)] 121
[character (DUAL)] BLEEKER (cont'd)
